latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director
investors@defidevcorp.co.uk
First Sentinel (Corporate Adviser)

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds AI‑driven sales assistants that help businesses automate outreach, follow‑ups and lead qualification. Their flagship product, Eve, is an advanced AI sales agent that learns from existing sales data and can generate personalized outreach sequences.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global AI‑sales‑automation market is projected to hit £30 billion by 2030. By turning human‑driven sales into a repeatable, data‑driven process, Cykel AI offers a product that can cut sales cycle time and lift revenue for mid‑size B2B companies. Their technology sits at the intersection of AI and sales, a space where early movers can lock in long‑term contracts.
company_30s.current_state: Cykel AI is still in its growth‑phase, having raised £2.8 m and a £750 k secondary placement to fuel product development. The company has launched two AI products (Eve and GTM AI) and announced a Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy to diversify its balance sheet. Revenue figures are not publicly disclosed, suggesting a pre‑revenue or very early‑revenue stage. The stock trades at a price that is a fraction of the company’s valuation, reflecting a steep discount to any future earnings potential.

rns_narrative: Cykel AI has been busy raising capital and repositioning itself. The latest filings show a £2.8 m equity raise, a £750 k secondary placement, and a name change that signals a broader strategic pivot. The company is also experimenting with a Bitcoin treasury reserve, a bold move that could give it a unique hedge against inflation.

Product‑centric news is the other major theme. Two AI sales tools – Eve, an advanced sales agent, and GTM AI – have been launched in the past six months. These releases suggest that the company is finally moving from concept to execution, which could attract institutional interest.

Governance updates – board changes, a major holdings notification, and a change of name – indicate the company is tightening its structure in preparation for a potential public‑market push or a strategic partnership.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent hope – the community is quiet but looking for a spark of traction.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the Bitcoin treasury move and the launch of two AI products. Traders see these as signals that the company is finally building something that can generate cash flow.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices point to the lack of revenue, the heavy reliance on capital raises, and the company’s thin operating history. They fear that without a clear path to profitability the stock will continue to languish.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has shifted from pure pessimism (no news, no revenue) to cautious optimism after the product launches, but the community remains largely silent – a classic contrarian signal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest, meaning the market is largely unaware of any upcoming catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A closing of the £2.8 m equity raise or a new funding round could provide the liquidity needed for scaling sales.
A partnership announcement with a mid‑size enterprise or a signed contract for the Eve platform would give the market a tangible revenue indicator.
Any announcement of a strategic alliance or a new customer that is publicly disclosed could trigger a volume spike.
A price break above the 10‑day moving average on a volume that is at least 1.5× the 20‑day average would confirm that the market is moving beyond the capitulation zone.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor daily volume for a 2× increase, look for a price break of 2–3 p above the most recent low, and keep an eye on any RNS that confirms a funding close or a new customer win.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle – capitulation may be complete but accumulation is still slow. The APEX compression score (if >8) would suggest a tight spring ready to pop once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company secures additional capital and signs even a single mid‑size customer, the price could rebound to the mid‑range of its 52‑week low. At current levels, a move from 5 p to 20 p would represent a 300 % upside – a classic asymmetric payoff for a deep‑crash play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no proven revenue stream and is still heavily dependent on equity funding. A failure to close a new round or to secure a key customer could push the stock back into a deeper trough, potentially eroding the remaining equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price (5 p) if the stock stalls. Potential gain could be 3–4× the entry price if the company moves to 20–25 p. The asymmetry is clear: high upside, limited downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce that clears the 10‑p support with at least 1.5× the 20‑day volume. Size the position so that the stop at 5 p is 5 % of the trade value, keeping risk below 10 % of your account. Re‑enter on a pullback to the 10‑p level if volume remains healthy.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear catalyst: a funding close, a signed customer contract, or a volume spike above 1.5× the average. If any of these trigger, re‑evaluate the entry level.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, gets delisted, or the board announces a liquidation, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cykel AI is a deeply discounted AI‑sales startup that has just started to show product traction and a bold Bitcoin treasury move; if it closes a new funding round or lands a mid‑size customer, the upside is huge, but the risk of continued decline remains until a clear revenue signal emerges.
